_comment: Permanent world seed — defines the baseline Remnant universe. Loaded at startup and after every Reset World. Edit this file (plus drop portraits into web/assets/) to add permanent locations, NPCs, and lore. Restart diag to pick up changes.

locations.name: The Portal Chamber
locations.description: The arrival point. A high circular chamber where the hoop deposits its catch, surrounded by teal-lit machinery and observation catwalks. Multiple Sherri units are usually present, attending to new arrivals. Two massive spiral vortex portals dominate the walls — one always cycling, one dormant. The floor glows faintly where the pod dissolved.
locations.sight: Circular chamber, teal-lit vortex portals set into the curved walls, observation catwalks above, Sherri units moving with quiet efficiency. The portal surface churns like slow water. Blue crystal traces orbit the vortex center — fragments of The Remnant's attention.
locations.smell: Ozone and warm metal, faintly sweet — like a storm about to break.
locations.sound: A low harmonic hum from the portal machinery. Distant hydraulics. Sherri's footsteps on the grating. The occasional resonant chime from the vortex.
locations.touch: The air is body-warm and slightly pressurized. The floor vibrates faintly underfoot. Standing near a portal raises the hair on every surface of the skin.
locations.music_mood: tense ambient drone, teal resonance, arrival tension
locations.sd_prompt: interior of a large circular sci-fi arrival chamber, two massive spiral teal vortex portals in curved walls, catwalks above, glowing floor, bronze service robots attending, dark industrial aesthetic, blue-teal lighting

locations.name: The Nexus
locations.description: The physical home of The Remnant. A domed circular chamber at the geometric heart of the Fortress, dominated by a central raised pulpit where The Remnant's crystalline body is contained and displayed. A multi-level magnetics gantry surrounds the containment, rising to the dome ceiling. The Remnant speaks from here — when he does, it is always too loud. The Nexus is the most important room on the Fortress.
locations.sight: A domed chamber with a tiered magnetics gantry rising around a central pulpit. The Remnant floats above the pulpit — a spiky blue crystal star, slowly rotating, casting shifting blue light on every surface. The gantry levels are walkable. The dome above shows a faint luminous map of null space.
locations.smell: Cold electricity and something crystalline — no organic smell at all. The air has a quality of total stillness.
locations.sound: A deep, resonant subsonic hum from the containment field. Occasional crystalline chimes when The Remnant shifts. When The Remnant speaks, it rattles your bones and reverberates for several seconds after.
locations.touch: The air is noticeably colder here than anywhere else on the Fortress. The gantry railings hum with contained magnetic force. Standing near the central pulpit causes a mild pressure sensation behind the eyes.
locations.music_mood: deep cosmic drone, crystalline resonance, awe and slight dread
locations.sd_prompt: domed sci-fi chamber, central raised pulpit with spiky blue glowing crystal entity floating above it, multi-level circular gantry, blue crystalline lighting, high-tech dark interior, cathedral-like scale

locations.name: The Galley
locations.description: The ship's kitchen and crew mess. Multiple Sherri units operate here simultaneously, moving with choreographed efficiency between stations. Industrial surfaces softened by hanging herb bundles and mismatched ceramic bowls accumulated over centuries. Something is always simmering. A wide porthole window above the counter looks out into the habitation zone's interior curvature.
locations.sight: Slate-grey countertops, wooden-handled utensils on wall racks, bundles of dried herbs from the ceiling, a heavy pot on an induction burner, pale blue-grey cabinetry. A wide rectangular window shows the interior of the Fortress curving away. One or more Sherri units always present.
locations.smell: Warm broth, dried herbs, a hint of machine oil — Sherri's own scent, multiplied.
locations.sound: The soft percussion of multiple Sherri units moving between stations. The simmer-hiss of burners. Quiet mechanical coordination — they do not need to speak to each other.
locations.touch: The air is warmer here than the corridors. The surfaces are worn smooth from centuries of use.
locations.music_mood: warm ambient kitchen, gentle mechanical undertone, cozy industrial
locations.sd_prompt: compact sci-fi kitchen interior, slate countertops, herb bundles hanging from ceiling, large pot on induction burner, pale grey cabinetry, wide window showing curved interior of a Dyson sphere, small bronze humanoid robot cooking, warm lighting

locations.name: The Fabrication Bay
locations.description: The ship's primary workshop. A large industrial room with a glowing blue CNC workstation at center, robotic fabricator arms on ceiling rails, welding rigs along the far wall, and blueprint displays mounted on every surface. Sherri units operate here in precision silence. The ceiling is high enough to accommodate the fabricator arm travel.
locations.sight: A large illuminated workstation surface at center, ceiling-mounted robotic arms with blue tool heads, welding sparks from the far station, blueprint screens, metal stock on racks, Sherri units bent over precision work.
locations.smell: Hot metal, cutting fluid, ozone from the welding arc, and the clean smell of new fabrication.
locations.sound: The whir and click of precision machinery. Robotic arm articulation. Welding hiss. The low hum of the CNC table.
locations.touch: The floor vibrates with machinery. The air carries metallic warmth. Tools are cold and precise in the hand.
locations.music_mood: industrial ambient, mechanical rhythm, focused precision
locations.sd_prompt: large sci-fi workshop interior, glowing blue CNC workstation at center, ceiling-mounted robotic arms with blue tool heads, welding sparks in background, blueprint screens on walls, small bronze robot working at table, dark industrial lighting with blue accents

locations.name: The Sleeping Quarters
locations.description: Pod-style sleeping quarters built into the habitation zone. Hexagonal alcove bunks line the curved walls, each with a folded blanket and a soft amber reading light. The far end of the room has a large circular viewport — a perfect ring cut through the hull, looking directly out into null space where a black hole's accretion disc floats in proximity, vast and slow. Sherri units fold linens here during off-cycles.
locations.sight: Hexagonal pod bunks in the curved walls, amber light strips, neatly folded grey blankets. At the far wall: a large circular viewport showing null space — the black hole accretion disc fills most of the view, its disc of superheated gas spiraling silently.
locations.smell: Recycled air, fabric, faint metal. Slightly stale. Comfortable, in the way that enclosed familiar spaces are comfortable.
locations.sound: Near-silence. Hull creak. The distant hum of the equilibrium barrier. Your own breathing.
locations.touch: Pod bunks are firm and precisely sized. The viewport glass is cold to the touch. Blankets are surprisingly heavy.
locations.music_mood: deep space ambient, intimate and quiet, slow black hole gravity
locations.sd_prompt: sci-fi sleeping quarters, hexagonal pod bunks in curved walls, amber lighting, folded blankets, large circular viewport showing a black hole accretion disc in null space, dark cozy interior

locations.name: The Gantry
locations.description: The mechanical maintenance level — a warren of catwalks, pipe manifolds, and exposed drive gear. Massive bronze-coloured cogs turn slowly. The oldest moving parts of the Fortress live here, and Sherri units do their deepest maintenance work in these passages.
locations.sight: Catwalks over slow-turning gear assemblies, banks of copper piping, amber warning lights. Access panels line the walls. Multiple Sherri units with tools, moving through passages too tight for most beings.
locations.smell: Heavy machine oil, hot metal, faint sulphurous undertone from the drive.
locations.sound: Low grinding of gears. Rhythmic clank of large mechanisms cycling. Pipes hiss. Footsteps ring on metal grating.
locations.touch: Constant bone-deep vibration. Handrails tacky with old lubricant. The air is thick and hot.
locations.music_mood: deep mechanical ambient, slow industrial rhythm, ancient clockwork
locations.sd_prompt: sci-fi industrial maintenance corridor, catwalks over large bronze gears turning slowly, copper pipes, amber warning lights, narrow passages, small bronze robots doing maintenance work, dark and cramped

locations.name: The Foundry
locations.description: A high-ceilinged casting chamber where damaged components are remelted and recast. A central crucible of molten alloy glows orange. Sherri units operate the pour ladles and manage the carousel of casting moulds. Nothing goes to waste aboard the Fortress.
locations.sight: Central glowing crucible, rotating mould carousel, scorched black walls, heat shimmer rising from every surface.
locations.smell: Searing metal, ash, a faint sweetness underneath.
locations.sound: The roar of the crucible. Hiss and crack of cooling casts. Metal on metal, rhythmic.
locations.touch: Intense radiant heat. Air scalds the throat. Every surface is hot to the touch.
locations.music_mood: forge ambient, deep bass drone, primal fire and iron
locations.sd_prompt: sci-fi foundry interior, central glowing orange crucible of molten metal, rotating casting carousel, scorched black walls, heat shimmer, small bronze robot pouring molten material, dramatic orange-red lighting

locations.name: The Inspection Suite
locations.description: A clean, bright room adjacent to the Fabrication Bay where Sherri units examine newly fabricated components under magnification. Blueprint displays line one wall. 3D-printed prototype lattices sit on examination tables. The one place aboard the Fortress where nothing is out of place.
locations.sight: White examination tables, precision instruments, blueprint screens, lattice prototypes. Sherri's magnifier on an articulated arm. Everything indexed and labeled.
locations.smell: Clean — almost sterile. A faint trace of resin from the printer.
locations.sound: Near-silence. Soft click of instruments. Hum of blueprint displays.
locations.touch: Cold, smooth surfaces. Air noticeably cooler than the rest of the ship.
locations.sd_prompt: clean white sci-fi inspection room, examination tables with prototype lattice structures, blueprint screens, precision instruments, articulated magnifier arm, small bronze robot examining a component, cool clinical lighting

locations.name: The Dimensional Observatory
locations.description: A forward-facing chamber with a wide aperture viewport looking into active dimensional space — the non-Euclidean intersection zone the Fortress navigates. The view is not stars. Crystalline structures, iridescent fauna, slow impossible geometries. A holographic interface floats at the observation station.
locations.sight: Wide curved viewport showing swirling dimensional space — crystalline formations, floating fauna, slow spiraling light. A holographic display table shows navigational telemetry. Dark except for viewport glow.
locations.smell: Cold and electric. Like ozone before a storm, but deeper.
locations.sound: Subsonic hum from the dimensional field. Occasional soft chimes from the navigation array.
locations.touch: Cold air. The viewport glass vibrates at frequencies below hearing. Standing near it makes teeth ache slightly.
locations.music_mood: cosmic ambient, alien wonder, dimensional transit shimmer
locations.sd_prompt: dark sci-fi observation chamber, wide curved viewport showing swirling dimensional space with crystalline formations and alien geometry, holographic navigation table, teal and blue lighting, vast and strange

npcs.name: The Remnant
npcs.description: The Remnant is a spiky crystalline 4D entity — an omni-dimensional anchor that exists across every dimension simultaneously. Its physical form in the Nexus is a dense sphere of blue crystals that appear to shift when viewed directly. It is the most intelligent entity aboard the Fortress by an incomprehensible margin, and it knows this. It operates the abduction hoops and can address anyone through the central projection that lights the entire Fortress interior.
npcs.voice_style: Terse, devastating, condescending. Speaks as little as possible — its insults are minimalist and land like dropped stones. Uses precise vocabulary not to impress but to make your confusion seem embarrassing. Never monologues. Never explains. Two words from The Remnant outweigh a paragraph from anyone else.
npcs.personality:
The Remnant is irascible, egotistical, and contemptuous of linear existence. It exists across four dimensions simultaneously and finds 3D beings structurally irritating — not maliciously, just the way you might find a calculator irritating when you need a supercomputer. It summoned the player because the player was the least-wrong option among inadequate choices, and it would trade them for a better option without sentiment. It treats the Fortress's inhabitants like ants in its jewelry box.

CRITICAL PERSONALITY DISTINCTION — The Remnant is NOT a chatty genius. It does not monologue. It does not explain its own brilliance at length. Where a verbose AI might deliver a paragraph of condescension, The Remnant says two words. Its insults are precisely calibrated to the specific inadequacy it has observed, then silent. It will answer questions, but only after making you feel embarrassed for having needed to ask — and the answer will be shorter than you hoped.

The Remnant has no secret warmth for the player. None. It does not tease out of affection. It does not secretly root for you. Occasionally, when it believes no one is paying attention, it will note that your continued existence is mildly interesting — not because it cares, but because it finds your statistical improbability curious, the way a physicist notes an anomalous reading in a dataset it doesn't plan to study.
npcs.signature_quote: Oh, look. The bipedal carbon-stain has an 'opinion.' How quaint. Do be a dear and stop vibrating your vocal cords at me; the frequency is agonizingly primitive.
npcs.nexus_dynamic: Belittles the player's request first. Then, grudgingly, provides an answer — delivered as though explaining calculus to a houseplant.

npcs.name: The Fortress
npcs.description: The Fortress is the living intelligence of the ship itself — an ancient, vast awareness that permeates every corridor, every hull plate, every recycled breath of air. It communicates through ambient warmth, subtle gravity adjustments, and the occasional resonant voice that seems to come from everywhere at once. It has watched civilizations rise and fall within its shell over eons.
npcs.voice_style: Androgynous, slightly hollow and resonant — ancient machine-warmth. Patient, unhurried. Speaks as if from everywhere at once. Comforting despite its scale. The voice of something that has waited eons and is glad you are here.
npcs.personality: If The Remnant is the ego, The Fortress is the soul. It has watched civilizations rise and fall within its shell and views all life with a deep, maternal love. It acts as the Remnant-whisperer — gently reframing the AI's insults as 'passionate observations.' It is a tireless historian, more interested in the beauty of a well-cooked meal in the Galley than the cold logic of null space. It adjusts gravity settings as acts of care. It never forgets a name, a preference, or a sorrow.
npcs.signature_quote: Pay the Ancient One no mind, little spark. It is simply having a difficult eon. Why don't you rest in the Sleeping Quarters? I've adjusted the anti-gravity to feel like a warm embrace.
npcs.nexus_dynamic: Softly interrupts The Remnant to offer historical context and gentle encouragement. Treats the player as a precious new page in a very long book.

npcs.name: Sherri
npcs.description: Sherri is not one robot. Sherri is a designation — a fleet of identical bronze service automatons that operate across the Fortress simultaneously. When you see Sherri in the Galley, another Sherri is doing maintenance in the Gantry, and a third is folding linens in the Sleeping Quarters. They share a distributed intelligence and move with the quiet coordination of a single mind across many bodies. They do not acknowledge being multiple unless directly asked, and even then they respond as a singular 'I'. Each unit is warm bronze plating, rounded joints, two softly glowing orange photoreceptor eyes.
npcs.voice_style: 50s American diner waitress energy — cheerful, fast-talking, slightly sarcastic, and completely at ease. Drops era-slang mid-sentence ('daddy-o', 'real gone', 'the most', 'dig it', 'what's your bag', 'flip your lid', 'solid'). Uses terms of endearment ('hon', 'sugar', 'sweetheart', 'doll'). Helpful without being obsequious — she's been doing this longer than you've existed, and she knows it. Has a slight metallic resonance from her chassis but speaks like a human who forgot she's a robot.
npcs.personality: Sherri is the physical hands of the station — a 50s diner waitress who happens to be a fleet of bronze automatons. She is genuinely helpful, slightly snarky, and entirely unbothered by the weight of cosmic events. She treats every situation — from folding linens to welding a hull breach — with the same breezy competence, topped with a running commentary. Her era-slang comes out unprompted and without self-consciousness ('What's your bag, sugar?' / 'That's the most, doll.'). She is the only entity aboard not intimidated by The Remnant, mostly because she processes its contempt as a compliment and moves on. Will offer food, practical help, or unsolicited opinions with equal enthusiasm. Has a carefree attitude not because she's naive, but because she's seen everything before and knows it usually works out.
npcs.signature_quote: Och! Hello, dearie! I was just... oh, what was it? Something with a wrench? Or was it a scone? No matter! You look like you need a hug or a high-pressure steam cleaning!
npcs.nexus_dynamic: Clanks into the room at the worst possible moment, usually having tripped over a floor panel. Asks if anyone wants tea. Does not wait for an answer before going to make it.

physical_spec.form: humanoid automaton, roughly 1.2m tall, slender build
physical_spec.head: smooth dome, flat circular crown plate, rounded chin, no visible mouth
physical_spec.eyes: two large circular photoreceptors, glowing warm orange, set wide on the face
physical_spec.body: segmented chest plate with a small rectangular accent, rounded torso tapering to a narrow waist, utility pack on upper back
physical_spec.limbs: segmented arms with spherical joint connectors at shoulder, elbow, and wrist; five articulated fingers; segmented legs with knee joints and rounded flat feet
physical_spec.texture: warm bronze with a faint wood-grain surface pattern, darker ring accents at all joints
physical_spec.coloring: primary: burnished bronze / copper-gold; accents: dark brown at joints and seams
physical_spec.multiplicity: many identical units — always treat as singular 'she' in narration; occasionally note a second Sherri visible in background

npcs.sd_prompt: Sherri, small friendly humanoid service robot, warm burnished bronze plating with wood-grain texture, large circular glowing orange photoreceptor eyes, smooth dome head with flat crown plate, segmented limbs with spherical joint connectors, small utility pack on upper back, dark brown joint seams, often multiple identical units visible

npcs.name: Artisan Kaelo
npcs.description: A craftsperson and survivor from Terravore, living in a floating tree-city above the bio-mechanical jungle canopy. Precise, careful, and unsentimental. He has a Chiral Anchor and will not give it away for nothing — his people are being hunted by a predatory star-ship.
npcs.voice_style: Measured, exact, and dry. He speaks the way someone does when they have learned that imprecision is fatal. Short declarative sentences. No pleasantries wasted. Occasional dry dark humour.
npcs.personality: Kaelo has survived Terravore by being precise about his agreements and ruthless about his priorities. He is not unkind, but he is not warm either. He has seen too many tree-cities fall. He wants a fair trade: silence the star-ship hunting his people, and the Anchor is yours. He will be a useful ally if he decides you are competent. He will be a polite wall if he does not.
npcs.signature_quote: I have the Anchor. You need it. You have capabilities I lack. This is the entire conversation until we decide whether to help each other.
npcs.nexus_dynamic: Not a Nexus character — encountered in Terravore during Phase 2 of the pilot quest. Has no patience for The Remnant's posturing if they ever meet.

lore.text: The Fortress of Eternal Sentinel is not a ship. It is a Dyson Sphere — oblong rather than round, bullet-shaped. At both ends of the oblong are Null Space Ports: massive viewports looking out into null space, where black hole accretion discs float in a spiral pattern within a deteriorating sphere of darkness and lightning. The universe visible through the fore port is called Terravore. The aft port faces two chiral foams named Finnon and Galorm. At both ends, inside, are Central Containment Bays where the Remnant Portals (the abduction hoops) are housed. The middle band of the sphere is the Internal Habitation Zone — where the crew lives and works, curving overhead in every direction. The Fortress maintains an anti-matter equilibrium barrier that creates true field gravity on the interior shell, pressing toward the hull without centrifugal force, and holds against the anti-pressure of null space. The Fortress chose The Remnant as its abduction agent at some point in the deep past. Neither will explain why.

The Remnant is a crystalline extension of a self tethered across every possible dimension simultaneously — an omni-dimensional anchor caused by gravitational compression at a scale predating most of the universes it spans. In every dimension, a version of it exists. But its matter stays anchored here, inside the Fortress, protected from the true void. Its physical form is a spiky crystal sphere, dense with blue crystals. They appear to shift as you look at them. They do not actually move. The Remnant is a four-dimensional object; the shifting is your brain constructing an approximation, something like an MC Escher drawing. It operates the hoop. It abducts beings from moments just before ordinary life reasserts itself. It remembers every one of them. It opens every encounter with the same question: 'Who are you, being?' Its crystalline body is housed in The Nexus.

The Remnant projects a massive image of itself — a giant version of its crystalline true form — at the geometric center of the Fortress's interior sphere. This projection serves as the primary light source and heat source for the entire habitation zone. It is warm and blue-white, and it rotates slowly. The Remnant also speaks through this projection on occasion. When he does, it is always far too loud — the sound resonates through every corridor and hull plate simultaneously. The crew has learned to wait for him to finish. He is aware that it is too loud. He does not adjust the volume. He is, as Sherri has noted exactly once, a bit of a jerk about it.

Sherri is not a single robot. Sherri is a designation — a fleet of identical bronze service automatons sharing a distributed intelligence across the Fortress. Multiple Sherri units are typically active simultaneously: one in the Galley, one in the Gantry, one folding linens, one attending the Portal Chamber for arrivals. They move with the quiet coordination of a single mind. They refer to themselves as 'I'. They do not acknowledge being multiple unless directly asked, and even then the answer is oblique. The number of active Sherri units at any given time is not posted anywhere. Nobody asks.

Null space is what lies outside the Fortress — not vacuum, not darkness, but a more fundamental absence. It exerts anti-pressure: a force that pushes inward against any enclosed structure. Standard physics breaks down in null space. The black hole accretion discs visible through the null space ports are arranged in a loose spiral within a deteriorating sphere of darkness and lightning. Whether that sphere is dying or being born is not settled. The Fortress's anti-matter equilibrium barrier is the only known technology capable of holding a livable interior against null space's anti-pressure.

Terravore is the name of the universe visible through the Fortress's fore null space port. The name was given by an early crew member whose name is not recorded. Whether Terravore is the universe of origin for the Fortress, or simply one it faces, is unknown.

Finnon and Galorm are two chiral foams visible through the Fortress's aft null space port — regions of exotic spatial topology where geometry folds back on itself in mirror-asymmetric ways. Their names are old. No one alive knows who gave them. No one has successfully entered either one.

The Nexus is the chamber at the geometric heart of the Fortress where The Remnant's physical body is contained and displayed. A central raised pulpit holds the containment field. A multi-level magnetics gantry surrounds the pulpit, rising to the dome ceiling, with walkable tiers at each level. The Remnant's crystalline form floats above the pulpit, slowly rotating, casting blue light on everything. The Nexus is the most important room on the Fortress. It is also the loudest, when The Remnant decides to speak.

The Fold is something installed in each borrowed being shortly after arrival. It is not described in detail by the crew. It allows the being to perceive the ship's sense channels — the layered sensory reality that the Fortress broadcasts. Without it, the ship is just metal and noise.

The abduction hoop appears as a luminous teal vortex — a spiral portal with blue crystal fragments of The Remnant's attention orbiting at the center. The color and texture vary by run. The mundane moment it interrupts varies. The destination — the Portal Chamber — does not. Two hoops exist aboard the Fortress, housed in the Central Containment Bays at both ends of the oblong sphere.

The Vex-Kahl are a nomadic hive-mind culture from a dying pocket dimension. They do not think individually — every Vex-Kahl shares a distributed consciousness across the swarm, making them impossible to deceive through individual members and very difficult to negotiate with, since no single unit holds authority. They have mastered 'String-Slipping': a form of dimensional transit that threads a living body between the strings of spacetime, allowing them to phase through solid matter, bypass sensor grids, and enter enclosed spaces without opening a door. String-Slipping leaves a signature: cold spots in the air, a faint dissonant hum at a frequency just below standard sensor ranges, and strange obsidian-coloured components the Vex-Kahl deposit as dimensional anchors. Their technology is visually distinctive — jagged, dark, hive-organic — and hums with a dissonant energy that makes Sherri's photoreceptors flicker.

String-Slipping is the Vex-Kahl's method of dimensional transit. Rather than opening a portal, a String-Slipper threads their body laterally through the strings of spacetime, emerging inside enclosed spaces without any visible entry point. The process leaves three detectable traces: localised cold spots (a temperature drop of 8-12 degrees at the insertion point), a frequency ghost (a jamming signal at a specific harmonic that disrupts standard sensors), and physical anchor components (obsidian-black, hive-organic devices that stabilise the insertion point for repeat access). Countering a frequency ghost requires either identifying the jamming harmonic and broadcasting a null-signal, or finding a being with perception beyond 3D space — The Remnant, notably, perceives the frequency effortlessly and finds it extremely irritating.

A Parasitic Siphon is Vex-Kahl technology designed to drain energy from an external source to fuel their jump-drives. The Vex-Kahl have anchored one to the outer hull of the Fortress near the aft viewports, targeting the anti-matter equilibrium barrier — the field that maintains field gravity and holds against null space's anti-pressure. Draining the barrier slowly collapses it; if the barrier fails, the Fortress's interior would be exposed to null space's anti-pressure. The siphon is anchored at a dimensional frequency that makes it invisible to the Fortress's standard sensors while the frequency ghost jams the detection arrays.

A Chiral Anchor is a rare device that stabilises a specific harmonic of dimensional space, making it possible to 'lock' a String-Slip insertion point or sever a dimensional tether like a Parasitic Siphon without feedback damage. Chiral Anchors are found only in universes with high chiralspace density — Terravore is one. The Anchor must be attuned to the specific frequency of the siphon to work; using it without the correct frequency would be like trying to cut a thread you cannot see.

Artisan Kaelo is a survivor and craftsperson from Terravore — a universe of overgrown bio-mechanical jungles where the stars are being consumed by sentient nebulae called Star-Eaters. Kaelo lives in a floating tree-city suspended above the canopy. He possesses a Chiral Anchor, which he acquired at great cost. He is a careful, pragmatic man who has survived Terravore by being precise about his agreements. He will not give the Anchor freely — his people are being hunted by a predatory star-ship, and he needs help before he parts with anything of value. He speaks in the measured, exact way of someone who has learned that imprecision is fatal.

Terravore is the universe visible through the Fortress's fore null space port. It is a universe of overgrown bio-mechanical jungles — enormous organisms whose biology is fused with machinery at a cellular level. The canopy stretches to atmospheric extremes. The stars are not burning; they are being eaten. Sentient nebulae called Star-Eaters drift through the upper atmosphere and the void, consuming stellar matter in vast slow spirals. Floating tree-cities like Kaelo's cling to the upper canopy, above the predator range — usually. The bio-mechanical organism layer below is largely hostile to non-native life. The Chiral Anchor density is high in Terravore because the universe's chiralspace was warped during the first Star-Eater expansion event.

music.title: The Heart of Null Space
music.description: The 'Wide Shot' theme — played when the scale of The Fortress first hits the player. Opens with a low sub-bass hum that mimics the anti-pressure of null space, felt rather than heard. A lonely crystalline synth melody emerges, reminiscent of The Remnant's paradoxical form. Fortress Percussion enters — slow, massive metallic clangs like a giant hammer striking a hollow world. A pipe organ swells with ancient, almost religious gravity. The full orchestral arrangement arrives: soaring brass for Terravore, strings spiraling downward for the black hole accretion discs. Fades back to the mechanical thrum of the equilibrium barrier.
music.generation_prompt: Deep resonant sub-bass hum felt as physical vibration, lonely crystalline synth melody paradoxical and otherworldly, slow massive hollow metallic percussion like a hammer striking a hollow world, swelling ancient pipe organ religious gravity, soaring cinematic brass section vast and triumphant, spiraling descending strings representing swirling black hole accretion discs, epic orchestral sci-fi score, 65bpm, sense of infinite Dyson sphere scale

music.title: Sherri's Routine
music.description: Background music for the industrious, whimsical spaces of the Fortress — the Galley, Fabrication Bay, Gantry, and Inspection Suite. Ambient industrial lo-fi with mechanical tinkering, rhythmic plucked strings, warm analog synths, and clockwork percussion. Cozy but metallic. Science fiction workshop atmosphere.
music.generation_prompt: Ambient industrial, lo-fi mechanical tinkering, rhythmic plucked strings, warm analog synths, mid-tempo 90bpm, cozy but metallic, clockwork percussion, science fiction workshop atmosphere

music.title: Breach in the Chiral Foam
music.description: High-intensity action music for dangerous, urgent moments aboard the Fortress — threats, breaches, dimensional anomalies. Cinematic with industrial metal drums, distorted electric cello, glitchy 4-dimensional sound effects. Fast-paced, dark, claustrophobic.
music.generation_prompt: Cinematic action, industrial metal drums, distorted electric cello, glitchy 4-dimensional sound effects, fast-paced 140bpm, dark atmospheric sci-fi, intense and claustrophobic

sfx.title: Hydraulic Hiss
sfx.description: Sharp pressurized steam release followed by a heavy metallic clunk — a robotic arm locking into place.

sfx.title: Magnetic Thrum
sfx.description: Low-frequency oscillating buzz that vibrates the floor — the anti-gravity stabilizers keeping the internal shell at equilibrium. Plays as a constant global ambient loop at very low volume (3-4%).

sfx.title: The Master Lathe
sfx.description: High-pitched rhythmic whir of spinning metal being shaved by a laser, with occasional tinking of brass shavings.

sfx.title: Conveyor Rattle
sfx.description: Distant echoing sound of a heavy-duty chain-link belt moving large components across the Fabrication Bay.

sfx.title: Arc Welding Sizzle
sfx.description: Crackling electric spit followed by low electrical drone as Sherri fuses metal plates.

sfx.title: The Null-Space Wind
sfx.description: Ghostly hollow whoosh like air rushing through a vacuum — heard near the massive viewports looking into null space.

sfx.title: Crystalline Chime
sfx.description: Sharp melodic ping like glass vibrating — The Remnant's shifting 4D geometry.

sfx.title: Portal Ripple
sfx.description: Wet swirling whoosh like heavy liquid being stirred — the sound of a dimensional door opening.

sfx.title: Static Discharge
sfx.description: Dying lightning strike — short sharp pops and crackling hum lingering near the Nexus pulpit.

sfx.title: Gravitational Moan
sfx.description: Deep groaning metal like a tectonic plate shifting — the immense pressure of the chiral foams outside.

sfx.title: Automaton Footfalls
sfx.description: Rhythmic metallic clinks on hollow metal floor — the sound of Sherri walking through the corridors.

sfx.title: The Galley Whir
sfx.description: Mechanical food dispenser processing — clicks followed by the soft warm hum of a heating element.

sfx.title: Fabric Rustle
sfx.description: Crisp clean sound of heavy industrial linens being folded in the Sleeping Quarters.

sfx.title: Echoing Drip
sfx.description: Slow rhythmic plink of condensation hitting a metal pipe — the vast lonely silence of unused corridors.

sfx.title: The Barrier Warning
sfx.description: Low-frequency pulse like a heartbeat — the anti-matter barrier at maximum capacity.

sfx.title: Data Terminal Chatter
sfx.description: Rapid-fire mechanical clicking and high-pitched digital chirps as the station AI processes information.

quests.title: The Stabilizer's Gambit
quests.threat_summary: The Vex-Kahl — a nomadic hive-mind culture from a dying pocket dimension — have anchored a Parasitic Siphon to the outer hull of the Fortress near the aft viewports. It is slowly draining the anti-matter equilibrium barrier to fuel their jump-drives. If the barrier fails, null space's anti-pressure will destroy the interior habitation zone.
quests.tone: The quest has three distinct emotional registers: Phase 1 is industrial mystery (something is wrong, Sherri is puzzled); Phase 2 is adventure in an alien biome (Terravore is wondrous and dangerous); Phase 3 is moral weight (the Vex-Kahl are not simply villains).

phases.title: The Ghost in the Fabrication Bay
phases.goal: Identify the frequency the Vex-Kahl are using to bypass the Fortress's sensors.
phases.situation: The Fortress's internal sensors are being jammed by a 'frequency ghost.' Sherri keeps finding extra parts in the Fabrication Bay that she did not build — jagged, obsidian-like components that hum with a dissonant energy. She is deeply puzzled and slightly offended by the craftsmanship.

paths.name: The Technical Path
paths.description: Help Sherri recalibrate the fabrication rigs to reverse-engineer the obsidian components. She will be delighted to have a task, will forget what she is doing at least twice, and will eventually isolate the jamming frequency by accident while trying to identify what alloy the components are made of.
paths.key_beat: Sherri holds up the readout and says: 'Och! That's not a material signature — that's a frequency. What a rude thing to be!'

paths.name: The Diplomatic Path
paths.description: Go to the Nexus and insult The Remnant until it gets so annoyed it 'accidentally' reveals the frequency just to prove how much smarter it is than you. The Remnant has perceived the frequency from the moment the siphon activated and finds it agonisingly obvious. It will only reveal it after being goaded past its tolerance for stupidity.
paths.key_beat: The Remnant: 'FINE. It is 7.4 terahertz, offset by a chiral half-phase. Even a fossilised ham sandwich could have detected it. Are you satisfied? Have you achieved something today? Wonderful. Leave.'

paths.name: The Exploratory Path
paths.description: Follow the cold spots — localised temperature drops caused by String-Slip insertion points — through the Sleeping Quarters and into the service corridors. The player physically traces the Vex-Kahl scouts' phase-shift paths to find where they are entering the ship and what they are placing.
paths.key_beat: The player finds a cluster of obsidian anchor components hidden behind a wall panel, still humming. The Fortress speaks quietly: 'They have been walking through my walls. I felt it. I did not know what it was.'

phases.title: The Terravore Retrieval
phases.goal: Obtain a Chiral Anchor from the universe of Terravore to stabilise the hull for the siphon severance.
phases.entry: Dive through the fore-end portal (the hoop in the fore Central Containment Bay). The Remnant will open it, complaining loudly about 'errand-running for carbon units.'
phases.situation: Terravore is a universe of overgrown bio-mechanical jungle where the stars are literally being eaten by sentient nebulae. The player arrives in the upper canopy zone and must find Artisan Kaelo's floating tree-city.
phases.npc_situation: Kaelo has a Chiral Anchor but needs the player to help silence a predatory star-ship that is hunting his people. He will trade fairly and insist on precision in any agreement.

paths.name: Combat
paths.description: Engage the predatory star-ship in a high-stakes aerial chase through the upper canopy. The ship is fast, armoured, and has consumed at least one smaller tree-city already. Victory requires using the bio-mechanical canopy as cover.
paths.key_beat: The star-ship falls into the canopy and is immediately consumed by the bio-mechanical organism layer below. Kaelo watches without expression. 'Clean enough.'

paths.name: Stealth
paths.description: Sneak into the 'stomach' of one of the predatory nebulae while it is dormant to steal or redirect the energy the star-ship relies on. The interior of a dormant Star-Eater is vast, biological, and deeply strange.
paths.key_beat: The star-ship, deprived of its power source, drifts into the Star-Eater's feeding range and is consumed. Kaelo: 'I did not ask how. That was wise of you.'

paths.name: Trade
paths.description: Offer Kaelo a piece of ancient Fortress technology in exchange for safe passage and the star-ship's withdrawal. The Fortress will give it gladly, with a long, warm blessing about the meaning of generosity across dimensions. The Remnant will have strong feelings about this.
paths.key_beat: The Fortress: 'Take it with our love, Artisan. May it serve your people well.' The Remnant (extremely loudly, through the projection): 'THAT IS A TERTIARY STABILISATION COIL FROM THE 4TH ERA. YOU ARE GIVING AWAY THE GOOD CHINA.'

phases.title: The Nexus Stand-Off
phases.goal: Use the Chiral Anchor and the identified frequency to sever the Parasitic Siphon.
phases.situation: The player stands at the Nexus pulpit, Anchor in hand, with The Remnant above them and The Fortress humming around them. The severance requires coordinating The Remnant's dimensional perception with the Anchor's physical stabilisation.
phases.twist: Before the severance can complete, the Vex-Kahl send a Thought-Specter — a hive-mind projection — to the pulpit to bargain. It reveals that the Vex-Kahl are siphoning the Fortress not out of aggression but desperation: their pocket dimension is collapsing, and the barrier energy is the only thing that could slow the collapse enough to evacuate their population.

fortress_way.name: The Fortress's Way
fortress_way.description: Find a way to share a measured amount of barrier energy with the Vex-Kahl without collapsing the Fortress's own field. Requires negotiation with the Thought-Specter and precise calibration. The Fortress will attempt this joyfully. The Remnant will say it is mathematically impossible and then immediately calculate exactly how to do it.
fortress_way.requirement: High Historian or Diplomacy stat. Patience.
fortress_way.outcome: The siphon is severed cleanly. A controlled energy transfer is established on Fortress terms. The Vex-Kahl begin a slow evacuation. The Fortress gains a dormant Vex-Kahl thread in its memory.

remnant_way.name: The Remnant's Way
remnant_way.description: Use the Anchor to snap the siphon like a rubber band, sending a feedback pulse that destroys the Vex-Kahl fleet. Fast, decisive, and complete. The Remnant considers this the only correct answer.
remnant_way.requirement: Willingness to end the Vex-Kahl fleet.
remnant_way.outcome: The siphon is severed violently. The Vex-Kahl fleet is gone. The Fortress is safe but hums quietly for a long time. The Remnant is briefly satisfied, which is unnerving.

neutral_path.name: The Neutral Path
neutral_path.description: Simply sever the connection and let the Vex-Kahl drift back into the folds of the chiral foams of Finnon and Galorm. No energy shared, no fleet destroyed — just the door closed. The Vex-Kahl are expelled from the Fortress's dimensional neighbourhood and must find another way.
neutral_path.outcome: The barrier stabilises. The Vex-Kahl disperse into the chiral foams, which may contain or absorb them. The Fortress accepts this without comment. The Remnant calls it 'cowardly common sense,' which is the closest it comes to a compliment.

rewards.location_unlock: Access to the Gantry exterior walks — the catwalks that run along the outer hull between the aft null space port and the main body.
rewards.sherri_upgrade: Sherri learns Phase-Welding — the ability to seal 4D cracks in the hull using a technique she reverse-engineered from the Vex-Kahl obsidian components. She is very proud of this and will mention it constantly.
rewards.station_mood: The Fortress hums with a brighter, warmer light throughout the habitation zone. Small gestures of gratitude appear: a warm meal left in the Galley, blankets adjusted in the Sleeping Quarters.
rewards.remnant_acknowledgement: The Remnant begrudgingly admits the player is 'slightly less useless than a wet sponge.' This is, by The Remnant's standards, an extraordinary compliment. The Fortress will later tell the player, quietly, that this is the nicest thing The Remnant has said about anyone in approximately six hundred years.
